title: A letter and a mystery... - It All Started With Walt Disney | Facebook
text:
## It All Started With Walt Disney's Post

* * *

[](https://www.facebook.com/FriendsOfDisneyHistoryAndScholarship?__cft__\[0\]=AZbw9warLnImvmHobxGBwdcn_giRPgIQ3v1OaBVRaSuml42IHT1E_krdx5cfNuE9ouATCHTOoqXaZKjgtj4_nXJMdl2zR5QxsmovCMeyPYpKty6jg9iZVbdpvbVAP1IBi9Rh6smqz9m_SoVz7q7ncCey84CviEzhRgnoZw55kfRkRw&__tn__=%3C%3C%2CP-R)

### [**It All Started With Walt Disney**](https://www.facebook.com/FriendsOfDisneyHistoryAndScholarship?__cft__\[0\]=AZbw9warLnImvmHobxGBwdcn_giRPgIQ3v1OaBVRaSuml42IHT1E_krdx5cfNuE9ouATCHTOoqXaZKjgtj4_nXJMdl2zR5QxsmovCMeyPYpKty6jg9iZVbdpvbVAP1IBi9Rh6smqz9m_SoVz7q7ncCey84CviEzhRgnoZw55kfRkRw&__tn__=-UC%2CP-R)

[October 9, 2018](https://www.facebook.com/FriendsOfDisneyHistoryAndScholarship/posts/pfbid0zjvErJkqXaaawmmgRK9o6SvmSDVrAaMve5VnZmQqjpeKVbhao3UX9T9gkymttcUQl?__cft__\[0\]=AZbw9warLnImvmHobxGBwdcn_giRPgIQ3v1OaBVRaSuml42IHT1E_krdx5cfNuE9ouATCHTOoqXaZKjgtj4_nXJMdl2zR5QxsmovCMeyPYpKty6jg9iZVbdpvbVAP1IBi9Rh6smqz9m_SoVz7q7ncCey84CviEzhRgnoZw55kfRkRw&__tn__=%2CO%2CP-R) Â·

A letter and a mystery

Walt Disney purportedly penned a letter 62 years ago on Oct. 9, 1956 to the future (to be placed in a time capsule) from his Burbank, California, studio entitled: PREDICTION OF ENTERTAINMENT IN WORLD 50 YEARS FROM NOW.

Some people believe the letter might have been part of a brilliant promotional campaign for Brad Birdâs live-action âTomorrowlandâ film, but was that sci-fi, action-adventure even in preliminary production in 2006?

The earliest mention we could find about â1952â which became âTomorrowlandâ was 2010. The earliest post about Waltâs letter was June 12, 2006: [https://boingboing.net/2006/06/12/walt-disneys-1956-ti.html](https://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fboingboing.net%2F2006%2F06%2F12%2Fwalt-disneys-1956-ti.html&h=AT4yzGF4IZ2NIJMD-h3o0Oc7RP3b9oCWNBr2xk3sWiRzaQx3lnT6G9AIHmumCwEevQFHFgeGvFIvUY9El6MwtHN5AN5VbrTZZlsm6SpGjcNAulQ7936lD6US5Hwx9SrZ0w7pKYPtJzd5PNpR9T4A3iKx9R_yE4Sy&__tn__=-UK-R&c\[0\]=AT4nQAqUWQBPh1DqHtAi48t8s3YRDGogk0zZoolj6RoTbcJunLo7DfvQolX8fsd0vM-Hl22uji-Ul5zEXUkyt0eLiPs4peX16pCPZDHfj9pcbaXqwv2hnL65vvfiXgX5Gx85GvXqlClLC24e5HvwHsPqgRDT0ErWBflOIH_9_OGll7s2_G27yKEVfQW4NQI)

Disney historian Jeff Kurtti wrote last year: âThe contents (of the letter) are authentic. The image is a ârecreation.â It was published in the Company newsletter, Disney Newsreel, in 2006.â

Highlights from Waltâs letter:

â... People will need and demand amusement, refreshment and pleasant relaxation from daily tasks as much in your day as they have in ours and in all the generations of mankind into the remote past. What the exact nature and implementation of these mass entertainments may be, doesn't make much difference, it seems to me.

âHumanity, as history informs us, changes very slowly in character and basic interests. People need to play as much as they need toil. They never cease to be fascinated by they own powers and passions, their base or noble emotions, their faiths and struggles and triumphs against handicap â all things that make them laugh and weep and comfort one another in love and sacrifice out of the depths of their being...â

[](https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1827998800588656&set=a.601722544679356&__cft__\[0\]=AZbw9warLnImvmHobxGBwdcn_giRPgIQ3v1OaBVRaSuml42IHT1E_krdx5cfNuE9ouATCHTOoqXaZKjgtj4_nXJMdl2zR5QxsmovCMeyPYpKty6jg9iZVbdpvbVAP1IBi9Rh6smqz9m_SoVz7q7ncCey84CviEzhRgnoZw55kfRkRw&__tn__=EH-R)

Clever publicity campaign, that is all.

* [7y](https://www.facebook.com/FriendsOfDisneyHistoryAndScholarship/posts/pfbid0zjvErJkqXaaawmmgRK9o6SvmSDVrAaMve5VnZmQqjpeKVbhao3UX9T9gkymttcUQl?comment_id=1828090093912860&__cft__\[0\]=AZbw9warLnImvmHobxGBwdcn_giRPgIQ3v1OaBVRaSuml42IHT1E_krdx5cfNuE9ouATCHTOoqXaZKjgtj4_nXJMdl2zR5QxsmovCMeyPYpKty6jg9iZVbdpvbVAP1IBi9Rh6smqz9m_SoVz7q7ncCey84CviEzhRgnoZw55kfRkRw&__tn__=R\]-R)

View 1 reply

Top fan

Mike Roberto

Publicity campaign or not Walt's vision of the future was always positive. I remember being in Disney World in 1973 and reading or hearing him speak about shorter work weeks and more leisure time. He really believed that we as people could go on and continue to do better. If folks weren't so greedy his vision could have happened. I think Walt would be sad if he could see what the world is now.
